quranicRelevance: Conviction of faith (Yaqeen), certainty of Hereafter, and death (referred to as al-Yaqeen - 'hat-ta ya'tiyaka al-yaqeen').

keyDerivations.meaning: Certainty, conviction, absolute truth, or death
To believe with certainty, or be convinced

figurativeOrRareUsages: عِلْمَ اليَقِينِ (102:5): The knowledge of certainty.
حَتَّى يَأْتِيَكَ اليَقِينُ (16:99): Until there comes to you the certainty (meaning death, which is absolutely certain to come).
discardedNoise: Epistemological debates in modern philosophy of science.
Obscure details on water settling in a stone hollow.
summaryEn: The root denotes absolute certainty, defining the highest level of conviction in faith and serving as a metaphor for death (Yaqeen).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على اليقين والقطع، واصفاً أعلى درجات الإيمان (يقين) ومستعملاً كناية عن الموت لحتميته.
rawText: أَيْقَنَ 4 see 1. إِسْتَيْقَنَ 10 see 1. تَيَقَّنَ 5 see 1. يَقِنَهُ 1 , (S, Msb, K,) and يَقِنَ بِهِ; (Msb;) and 🡻أَيْقَنَهُ, (S, K,) and 🡻ايقنبِهِ; (K;) and 🡻تَيَقَّنَهُ; and 🡻اِسْتَيْقَنَهُ, (S, Msb, K,) and 🡻استيقن بِهِ; (K;) all signify the same; (S;) He knew it; he was, or became, certain, or sure, or he made sure, of it; syn. عَلِمَهُ; (Msb, K;) intuitively, and inferentially; (Msb;) [see 1 in art. علم;] and تَحَقَقَّهُ. (K.) يَقِينٌ is of the measure فَعِيلٌ in the sense of the measure فَاعِلٌ, signifying Sure, or certain, and manifest. (Msb.) === And knowledge, or certainty; [see ظَنٌّ;] instinctive and inferential. (Msb.) === عَيْنُ اليَقِينِ: see عَيْنٌ (latter part). === الحقُّ اليَقِينُ and حَقُّ اليَقِينِ: see جَامِعٌ; where the two words are said to have the same meaning: and Bd and Jel in lvi. 95; in the latter of which, اليقين in حَقُّ اليَقِينِ is expressly said to be an epithet: see also Ham, p. 335.
